steps: Prepare 10% (vol/vol) Triton X-100 Combine 90 mL of NFW with 10 mL of Triton X-100 Store at Room temperature for at least 6 months
Prepare 10% (vol/vol) Tween-20 Combine 90 mL of NFW with 10 mL of Tween-20 Store at Room temperature for at least 6 months
Prepare 5 M NaCl Dissolve 291.65 g of NaCl in 700 mL, approx. of NFW Adjust final volume to 1 L Store at Room temperature for at least 1 year
Prepare 1 M KCl Dissolve 74.55 g of NaCl in 900 mL, approx. of NFW Adjust final volume to 1 L Store at Room temperature for at least 1 year

Prepare 20% (vol/vol) Perfluorooctanol in HFE-7500 oil (20% (vol/vol)) - 20% PFO Perform this step in a fume-hood with appropriate personal protective equipment and avoid contact with skin and eyes when handling this reagent. Store the resulting mixture in a fume cupboard. Combine 8 mL of HFE-7500 oil with 2 mL of Perfluorooctanol Store at Room temperature for at least 1 year
Prepare 50 mL, approx. of DNA elution buffer %s %s Make into 1 mL aliquots and store them at -20 °C for up to 1 year.
Prepare 500 mL of TET buffer %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at Room temperature for up to 1 year
Prepare 1 L of TBSET buffer %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at Room temperature at least 6 months

Prepare 100 mL of 1% (vol/vol) Span-80 in hexane Perform this step in a fume-hood with appropriate personal protective equipment and avoid contact with skin when handling this reagent. Store the resulting mixture in a fume cupboard. Combine 99 mL of hexane with 1 mL of Span-80 Store at Room temperature in a fume cupboard for at least 6 months
Prepare 1 L of HB wash buffer %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at Room temperature at least 6 months
Prepare 1 L of STOP-25 buffers %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at Room temperature at least 6 months Prechill to 4 °C before use.
Prepare 1 L of STOP-10 buffer %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at Room temperature at least 6 months
Prepare 500 mL of Hybridization buffer %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at Room temperature at least 6 months
Prepare 500 mL of Neutralization buffer %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at Room temperature at least 6 months

Prepare 5 mL of 10% (wt/vol) ammonium persulfate (APS) Perform this step in a fume-hood with appropriate personal protective equipment and avoid contact with skin when handling this reagent. Combine 500 mg of ammonium persulfate with 4.5 mL of NFW Adjust final volume to 5 mL Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane Aliquots of 100 µL can be stored at -20 °C for up to 6 months

Prepare 4x of 10 mL of Acrylamide/bis-acrylamide (AB) solution Acrylamide and bis-acrylamide are potent neurotoxins. Perform this step in a fume-hood with appropriate personal protective equipment and avoid contact with skin and eyes when handling this reagent. %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at at least 6 months

5′-/acrydite/photocleavable spacer/CGATGACGTAATACGACTCACTATAG GGATACCACCATGGCTCTTTCCCTACA CGACGCTCTTC-3′ Resuspend and dilute to 250 micromolar (µM) in DNA Elution Buffer Acrydite-modified DNA primers are light-sensitive, as they contain a photocleavable (1-(2-nitrophenyl)-ethyl-based) moiety. At all times protect the DNA primers from exposure to UV light (<400 nm). Noticeable primer deactivation may occur at prolonged (≥20 min) exposure to ambient white light illumination. Perform in dim illumination and away from UV light sources. Store at -20 °C for at least 1 year

Prepare 250 mL of d enaturation solution. NaOH is highly corrosive; wear appropriate personal protective equipment when handling this reagent. Perform this step in a fume-hood with appropriate personal protective equipment and avoid contact with skin and eyes when handling reagents in this section. %s %s Filter solution through a 0.2 µm membrane. Store at Room temperature PREPARE FRESH
Prepare 2.5 mL of carrier oil mix Combine 1.25 mL of HFE-7500 oil with 1.25 mL of dSurf (2%) Vortex well Store at Room temperature and prepare freshly for the experiment run

Prepare 2.5 mL of TEMED mix Perform this step in a fume-hood with appropriate personal protective equipment and avoid contact with skin when handling this reagent. Combine 2.5 mL of carrier oil with 10 µL of TEMED Vortex well Store at Room temperature and prepare freshly for the experiment run

Prepare 1 mL of Acrylamide–primer mix in a 2 mL tube Acrylamide and bis-acrylamide are potent neurotoxins. Perform this step in a fume-hood with appropriate personal protective equipment and avoid contact with skin and eyes when handling reagents in this list. Acrydite-modified DNA primers are light-sensitive, as they contain a photocleavable (1-(2-nitrophenyl)-ethyl-based) moiety. At all times protect the DNA primers from exposure to UV light (<400 nm). Noticeable primer deactivation may occur at prolonged (≥20 min) exposure to ambient white light illumination. Perform in dim illumination and away from UV light sources. %s %s
Cut tubing for the following lengths using the template guides found in the template bag: 2 x lengths of each size of 1/32 3 x lengths of the 1/16 tube

Place Carrier oil/TEMED mix and Acrylamide–primer mix into p-valve system. Connect the sample tube to p-valve head Cover tube with foil Slide green spacer over 1/32 tube to center Thread green space and tube through the cream seal and place the blue plug in reverse over the green spacer facing the cream seal thread Place 1/32 tubing through the aluminium seal in top until it hits the bottom of tube Slide the green spacer and seals down until they hit the aluminium thread Screw seal thread until the 1/32 tubing does not move anymore Place both into tube stand
Vortex solution to mix and centrifuge - 1000 x g, Room temperature, 5 seconds
Place 500 µL of mineral oil on top of the solution
Place MF bead device onto microscope mount upside down and secure

Attach flow units and connect conversion adapter to 1/32 tubing coming from each p-valve Slide flow-unit seal over the 1/32 tube coming from p-valve and connect to LHS of flow unit. Tighten until the tube does not move anymore Attach the short 1/32 tubing to the RHS of the flow unit using the same method Attach red conversion adapter to the 1/16 tubing Attach 1/32 from RHS flow-unit to the other side of the conversion adaptor
Connecting tubing to MF device including output
Place p-valve head unit over collection tube and place into tube rack. Slide output tubing into top of p-valve but do not seal. Wrap output tube in foil.
Turn on power Turn on microscope lighting Connect the camera to a laptop and get a live picture

TODO
Remove the top (mineral oil) and bottom (carrier oil) phases from the 2-ml collection tubes, leaving 500 µL, approx. of polymerized HBs per tube. TODO: PICTURE
Add 500 µL of TBSET buffer on top of the creamy-looking emulsion. TODO: PICTURE
Release HBs from the emulsion by adding 1 mL of 20% PFO Vortex well then centrifuge at 5000 x g, Room temperature, 30 seconds HBs should appear as a milky (or semitransparent) phase. TODO: PICTURE

Remove the bottom 20% PFO phase. Repeat go to step #41 until the milky phase is transformed into a solid, well-packed, translucent mass on top of the 20% PFO phase. Remove the final bottom 20% PFO phase.

Add 1 mL of 1% (vol/vol) Span-80 in hexane Vortex well then centrifuge at 5000 x g, Room temperature, 30 seconds This time, the HBs will appear as a solid mass at the bottom of the tube. Remove the hexane layer. Repeat this step.
Add 1 mL of TBSET buffer and resuspend HBs by vortexing.
Transfer HBs to 15 mL tubes.

Fill tubes with TBSET buffer. Vortex then centrifuge at 3000 x g, Room temperature, 3 minutes The HBs will sediment whereas traces of hexane will appear as a milky layer on the top. Carefully aspirate the supernatant, keeping the tip of the serological pipette at the liquid-air interface. If the HBs do not sediment, extend the centrifuge time.
Repeat go to step #46 until the top milky layer disappears.
Pass the HBs through a 70 µm cell strainer on top of a 50 mL tube. Use ice-cold TBSET buffer to facilitate the passage of the beads through the mesh.
TODO: QC the bead size Put 20 microlitre on slide and check in microscope, take picture and use imageJ to check measurement - using python script for size of particles etc get a histogram
Transfer HB tubes to an incubator overnight at 65 °C protected from light.

Centrifuge at 3000 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes Remove supernatant. Transfer batches of HBs into 15 mL tubes and collect remaining into 50 mL tube. Wash the sides of the tube with 1 mL of TBSET buffer to release HBs stuck to the side for the 50 mL tube.
Store at 4 °C for up to 6 months protected from light.

Prepare FAM-PE1 probe 5′-/6-FAM/AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAG-3′ (standard desalting) Prepare a 100 micromolar (µM) solution in DNA elution buffer. This solution is stable for at least 1 year at -20 °C

Spin down hbs add 10 µL to 1.5 mL tubes and 1.4 mL of QC buffer Vortex Cent 1 min @ 1,000g remove supermatent adjust final QC buffer vol to 36 µL Add 4 µL of probe mix by pipetting up and down incubate room temp for 20 mins Wash x 3 - add 1.4ml of QC buffer, vortex, centrifuge 1 min @ 1000g - remove supernatant should have ~40 left in each tube Split into 2 tubes Place on slide and image expose other tube to UV place on slide and image
